bullets.text: At least 82 people have died in China's worst coal mining disaster in 16 years, following an explosion in Shanxi province.
The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is rapidly widening, with suspected cases tripling in a week, prompting the White House to pause the removal of detainees to the DRC.

Russian President Putin has vowed retaliation after accusing Ukraine of striking a student dormitory, while Ukraine claims to have hit Russia's elite Rubicon drone military unit in a Moscow-occupied region.

Yorke Peninsula businesses are expressing frustration over 18 months of blackouts, stating that SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan to fix the issue is long overdue and they have lost hundreds of thousands of dollars.

The Iran war has revealed significant vulnerabilities in global supply chains and exposed overlooked deficiencies in Australia's manufacturing sector, which are crucial for national resilience during a crisis.

A designer is suing Australian Shopify, alleging that the platform hosted 3,929 'ghost stores' that copied his work, in a rare attempt to hold global online platforms accountable for allegedly facilitating scam digital shopfronts.
Uber Technologies Inc. has reportedly offered to acquire Delivery Hero SE for approximately €10 billion, intensifying competition in the food delivery market.
The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge is nearing 4%, driven by a war-induced surge in energy costs, raising concerns about broader price pressures.
Economists are debating how to incorporate morality into economic models, considering it as more than just an external constraint.
